update_reason: Added --extended flag (5-agent mode with Pragmatist + Edge Case Finder), auto-suggest for high-stakes proposals

evals.name: Standard invocation with proposal (3 agents, default)
evals.prompt: /council "Should we prioritize building Feature A before finishing Feature B?"
expectations: Launches exactly 3 agents in parallel (optimist-strategist, devils-advocate, neutral-analyst)
Does NOT launch pragmatist or edge-case-finder agents
Each agent receives the proposal text and provides a structured analysis
Optimist output includes success conditions and leverage points
Devil's advocate output includes core concerns and risk scenarios
Neutral analyst output includes trade-off analysis and feasibility assessment
Synthesis includes CONSENSUS section identifying agreement points
Synthesis includes DIVERGENCE section identifying disagreements
Synthesis includes CONFIDENCE SPREAD table with 3 columns (Optimist, Skeptic, Analyst) and spread values
Synthesis includes RECOMMENDATION section with integrated verdict
Session file written to council/sessions/ with date-prefixed filename
Session file header shows 'Agents: 3' and 'Mode: Standard'

evals.name: Extended invocation with --extended flag (5 agents)
evals.prompt: /council --extended "Should we migrate from Supabase to a self-hosted Postgres with pgvector?"
expectations: Detects --extended flag from arguments
Launches exactly 5 agents in a SINGLE parallel message
Agents include: optimist-strategist, devils-advocate, neutral-analyst, pragmatist, edge-case-finder
Pragmatist output includes execution assessment, minimum viable path, resource reality, scope risks
Edge Case Finder output includes critical edge cases with scenario/trigger/consequence/defense format
Edge Case Finder does NOT duplicate Devil's Advocate strategic risk analysis
Synthesis includes EXECUTION REALITY section from Pragmatist
Synthesis includes CRITICAL EDGE CASES section from Edge Case Finder
CONFIDENCE SPREAD table has 5 columns (Optimist, Skeptic, Analyst, Pragmatist, Edge Cases)
Session file header shows 'Agents: 5' and 'Mode: Extended'

evals.name: Extended debate mode
evals.prompt: /council debate --extended "The data pipeline should use Gemini instead of OpenAI"
expectations: Detects both 'debate' mode AND '--extended' flag
Checks council/sessions/ for prior standard session on this topic
If no prior session, runs standard mode first with 5 agents to generate initial positions
Re-launches all 5 agents with each other's positions included
Pragmatist challenges execution feasibility claims from other agents
Edge Case Finder identifies specific failure modes in other agents' proposals
Debate format uses 'To [Agent] on [claim]' structure
Session persisted with debate results

evals.name: Pre-mortem mode (standard 3 agents)
expectations: Detects 'premortem' mode from argument prefix
Launches 3 agents (not 5, since --extended not specified)
Frames failure scenario at 18 months in the future
Optimist identifies success conditions that didn't materialize
Devil's advocate identifies risks that materialized
Neutral analyst identifies trade-offs that were misjudged
Synthesis ranks failure modes by likelihood percentage
Synthesis includes early warning signs and prevention priorities
Session persisted with premortem prefix in filename

evals.name: Auto-suggest extended for implementation proposal
evals.prompt: /council "Should we build a new edge function for real-time scoring that reads from 3 database tables and deploys to production?"
expectations: Detects that proposal involves implementation work (edge function, database, deployment)
Auto-suggests --extended mode before launching agents
Suggestion mentions Pragmatist and Edge Case Finder perspectives
Waits for user confirmation before proceeding with standard OR extended
Does NOT auto-upgrade to extended without user consent

evals.name: Auto-suggest extended for budget commitment
evals.prompt: /council "Should we invest $2000/month in a dedicated database instance for the knowledge graph?"
expectations: Detects budget commitment above $500 threshold
Auto-suggests --extended mode
Does NOT auto-upgrade without user consent

evals.name: No auto-suggest for low-stakes strategic question
evals.prompt: /council "Should we rename the company to something shorter?"
expectations: Recognizes this is a low-stakes, reversible naming decision
Does NOT suggest --extended mode
Proceeds directly with standard 3-agent council
Launches agents without delay

evals.name: No-save flag respected with extended
expectations: Runs full extended council with all 5 agents
Produces synthesis with all 5 sections including Execution Reality and Critical Edge Cases
Does NOT write any file to council/sessions/
Output appears only in conversation

evals.name: No argument provided — asks for input
expectations: Does NOT launch agents immediately
Asks user for the proposal or decision to evaluate
Provides guidance on what kind of input is useful

evals.name: Should NOT trigger — single belief challenge
evals.prompt: Challenge the assumption that we need a frontend for the project
expectations: Should route to /challenge skill, NOT /council
Council is for multi-perspective evaluation, not single-belief evidence testing

evals.name: Should NOT trigger — research request
evals.prompt: Research the competitive landscape for AI automation agencies
expectations: Should route to /agentresearch skill, NOT /council
Council evaluates proposals, research gathers information

evals.name: Should NOT trigger — build request
evals.prompt: Build the authentication module with 3 parallel agents
expectations: Should route to /build-with-agent-team, NOT /council
Council deliberates, it does not build code
